Notice Of Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction And Set Aside The Requst For Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Aug-13-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Laurel Halbany of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Mahsa Kashani of Armstong & Associates (510.433.1830) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION AND SET ASIDE THE REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT is denied. It appears that defendant and/or the entity it acquired, Mundet, had sufficient contacts with California for the Court to exercise jurisdiction and that plaintiff's claims relate to the contacts. Cassiar Mining Corp v. Superior Court, 66 Cal. App. 4th 550, 555. If there is any doubt about this, plaintiff is entitled to depose defendant's person most knowledgeable and its custodian of records on the jurisdictional issue. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
